repo_prior_art.cite: API Section 1 panel entries cover Q1 residualized bases and Markov spectral sign thresholds, not SDiD-ASCM-MC population comparison objects

published_prior_art.cite: ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021, AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021, and BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 define the separate estimators; common notation is routine

repo_prior_art.cite: API Sections 1.1, 1.2, and 1.4: Q1 minimal-basis, staggered minimal-basis, and spectral-threshold entries do not state an SDiD-ASCM-MC equality/separation certificate

published_prior_art.cite: Closest verified: ArkhangelskyImbens2022 double-robust panel ID; AAHIW2021 SDiD; AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 MC; none gives a necessary-and-sufficient equality frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: API Section 1 has sign/bias and spectral Q1 entries, but no pairwise first-order separation geometry for SDiD, ASCM, MC, and staggered weighting

published_prior_art.cite: Closest verified: FermanPinto2021 imperfect pretreatment fit and BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 ASCM; neither gives pairwise derivative gaps across SDiD-ASCM-MC-staggered functionals

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked tuple pins SDiD ridges and MC nuclear-norm penalty but no ASCM ridge/augmentation penalty, so ridge ASCM is not uniquely specified.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The MC functional is asserted to be a metric projection of H onto the donor pre-period row span, but nuclear-norm MC generally needs a completion/subgradient block; H's Riesz representer space is not defined tightly enough.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: phi_sdid is described as a single-cohort functional but is equated to the aggregate cohort-weighted tau_ATT when C>1; the pooled versus cohort-specific convention must be fixed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the ASCM augmentation penalty and normalization to p_IFE, then state whether rho_N/rho_T are SDiD-only or also enter ASCM's regression adjustment.
Replace the MC projection sentence with an explicit population optimization problem and its first-order/subgradient certificate, then rebuild K from that certificate.
Define phi_sdid,c, pooled phi_sdid, and phi_stag separately before Conjecture 1, so the C=1 and C>1 statements have unambiguous targets.
Add a boundary corollary for positive lambda_mc or positive ASCM ridge showing how the equality frontier changes away from the zero-penalty perfect-span case.

recommended_next_step: Revise the functional definitions and penalty tuple, especially the MC subgradient certificate and cohort-specific SDiD notation, then resubmit for novelty review.
